name: Betanzos - Melide Return
region_intro: Road cycling in Betanzos-Mandeo offers diverse terrain shaped by the Mandeo and Mendo Rivers, which flow into the Betanzos Estuary. The region features river valleys, coastal areas, and ancient Atlantic forests, providing varied gradients and scenic backdrops for no traffic road cycling routes. Cyclists can navigate paved surfaces through landscapes that range from the estuary's mudflats to inland forests and historic areas. This mix of natural features creates a dynamic environment for road cyclists of varying fitness levels. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Betanzos-Mandeo The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Betanzos – Ponte Vella loop from Betanzos-Cidade, a 41.9 miles (67.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain along river valleys and through historic areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Betanzos Velodrome – Chapel of San Cosme loop from Betanzos-Cidade, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.4 km) path. This route traverses diverse landscapes, including views of the Mandeo River and surrounding forests. Local road cyclists also love the Chapel of San Cosme loop from Betanzos-Cidade, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and past ancient forests, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Road cycling in Betanzos-Mandeo is defined by its river valleys, extensive estuaries, and ancient Atlantic forests. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Betanzos-Mandeo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available in Betanzos-Mandeo?
A: There are over 25 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes in Betanzos-Mandeo, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: The no-traffic road cycling routes in Betanzos-Mandeo offer a diverse terrain, from picturesque river valleys along the Mandeo and Mendo Rivers to coastal stretches near the Betanzos Estuary. You'll also find routes winding through ancient Atlantic forests and areas with varying gradients, providing a mix of challenges and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any easy no-traffic road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Betanzos-Mandeo offers a few easy no-traffic road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without excessive effort.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along these routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Mandeo River and the Betanzos Estuary. You might also pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5992088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2881257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrido Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its ancient Atlantic forests, providing a lush green backdrop to your ride.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Betanzos-Mandeo features several difficult no-traffic road cycling routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Betanzos – Ponte Vella loop from Betanzos-Cidade&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 67 km with more than 1300 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular no-traffic road cycling routes in Betanzos-Mandeo?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes in Betanzos-Mandeo are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39547729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of San Cosme loop from Betanzos-Cidade&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular option.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Betanzos-Mandeo?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Betanzos-Mandeo, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet, traffic-free roads, the diverse landscapes ranging from river valleys to coastal views, and the opportunity to explore historical towns and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near the historic center of Betanzos?
A: While the routes focus on no-traffic roads, many start or pass close to Betanzos. The historic center itself is a Historic-Artistic Site with well-preserved Gothic architecture, including churches like Santa María del Azogue. You can easily integrate a visit to the charming old town into your cycling trip.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Betanzos-Mandeo?
A: The region generally enjoys a mild climate. Spring and early autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can also be great, especially for coastal routes, though it might be busier. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the varied terrain of Betanzos-Mandeo provides several opportunities for scenic viewpoints. You might encounter spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3230837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Betanzos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/422013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint - Miño Castro Station Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the estuary and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Mandeo River area?
A: The Mandeo River is central to the region's natural beauty, and several routes follow its course or offer views of its picturesque valleys. The riverbanks feature riparian woodlands and granite formations, providing a beautiful setting for your ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39551125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Senses loop from Miño&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride with river views.
tips: Regional municipality of Camino de Santiago and EV routes (EuroVelo). Great midway place to eat and fill your bottles for the return trip
Located just before Sobrado, it is advisable to cool your feet in the pond and spend a few minutes in the shade of the trees.
